# MCR Compliance Checklists — By Forum

## 14th Circuit Court (Trial Level)

### Motion Filing (MCR 2.119)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Written motion with supporting brief | MCR 2.119(A)(1)-(2) | Caption, motion body, brief |
| Proposed order as separate document | MCR 2.602 | Included in stack |
| Certificate of service | MCR 2.107 | Method + date + addresses |
| Service ≥9 days before hearing | MCR 2.119(C)(1) | Compute from hearing date |
| +3 days if served by mail | MCR 2.107(C)(3) | Total ≥12 days if mailed |
| Verification/affidavit if facts outside record | MCR 2.114(A) | Sworn statement |
| Signature with P-number or Pro Se designation | MCR 2.114(B) | Block present |
| Caption format per MCR 2.113 | MCR 2.113(A)-(C) | STATE OF MICHIGAN header |
| 8.5"×11", 1" margins, 12pt font | MCR 2.113(C)(1) | Page formatting |
| Sequential page numbering | MCR 2.113(C)(1) | Bottom center preferred |

### Emergency Motion (MCR 2.119(F))

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| All regular motion requirements above | MCR 2.119 | Complete stack |
| Affidavit of irreparable harm | MCR 2.119(F)(1) | Sworn + specific |
| Explanation of emergency | MCR 2.119(F)(2) | Why normal scheduling insufficient |
| Notice to opposing party (even if ex parte) | MCR 2.119(F)(3) | Attempt documented |
| Certificate of efforts to notify | MCR 2.119(F)(3) | In CoS or separate |

### Custody/Parenting Time (MCR 3.206-3.210)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| **UCCJEA Affidavit (FOC 30)** | MCR 3.206(B)(1) | **MANDATORY — FATAL** |
| Verified statement of facts | MCR 3.206(B)(2) | Sworn |
| Best-interest factors addressed | MCL 722.23(a)-(l) | 12 factors |
| Friend of Court notification | MCR 3.208 | FOC has 21 days to respond |
| Parenting time specifics | MCL 722.27a | Detailed schedule |
| Child's established custodial environment | MCL 722.27(1)(c) | Burden analysis |

### PPO Proceedings (MCR 3.706-3.708)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Petition on SCAO form | MCR 3.706(A) | Correct form used |
| Affidavit supporting claims | MCR 3.706(B) | Sworn statement |
| Service within 182 days | MCR 3.706(H)(2) | Track service date |
| Motion to modify/terminate | MCR 3.707 | If contesting PPO |
| Contempt motion (if violated) | MCR 3.708(B) | Standard: beyond reasonable doubt |

## Michigan Court of Appeals (MCR 7.201-7.215)

### Appeal Brief (MCR 7.212)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Cover page with case title | MCR 7.212(A) | Formatted per rule |
| Table of Contents | MCR 7.212(B) | Page numbers for each section |
| Index of Authorities | MCR 7.212(B) | All cited cases/statutes with pages |
| Statement of Jurisdiction | MCR 7.212(C)(1) | Basis for appeal |
| Statement of Questions Presented | MCR 7.212(C)(2) | Concise, numbered |
| Statement of Facts | MCR 7.212(C)(5) | Record citations required |
| Arguments with headings | MCR 7.212(C)(6) | Each issue separate section |
| Relief requested | MCR 7.212(C)(7) | Specific remedy sought |
| Signature | MCR 7.212(C)(8) | Attorney/Pro Se |
| **50-page limit** | MCR 7.212(B) | Excludes tables/appendix |
| Appendix with key orders | MCR 7.212(C)(9) | Relevant lower court orders |
| Claim of appeal filed within 21 days | MCR 7.204(A)(1) | **JURISDICTIONAL** |

### Emergency Application for Leave (MCR 7.205)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Application with legal argument | MCR 7.205(A) | Brief format |
| Affidavit of urgency | MCR 7.205(F)(2) | Irreparable harm |
| Lower court order attached | MCR 7.205(A) | Copy of order appealed |
| Filing fee or fee waiver | MCR 7.205(A) | $375 or MC 20 |
| Proof of service | MCR 7.205(A) | On all parties |

## Michigan Supreme Court (MCR 7.301-7.306)

### Application for Leave to Appeal (MCR 7.305)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Statement of question | MCR 7.305(B)(1) | Why MSC should accept |
| Statement of facts | MCR 7.305(B)(3) | With record citations |
| Argument with authorities | MCR 7.305(B)(4) | Significance to jurisprudence |
| COA decision attached | MCR 7.305(B)(5) | If applicable |
| **13 copies** | MCR 7.305(A) | Physical copies |
| Filing fee $375 or waiver | MCR 7.305(A) | MC 20 form |

### Complaint for Superintending Control (MCR 7.304)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Extraordinary relief basis | MCR 7.304(A) | No adequate remedy at law |
| Verified complaint | MCR 7.304(B) | Sworn |
| Show cause: clear legal duty | MCR 7.304(B)(2) | Specific duty breached |
| Lower court orders attached | MCR 7.304(B)(3) | All relevant orders |
| All parties served | MCR 7.304(D) | Certificate of Service |

### Habeas Corpus (MCR 3.303-3.304)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Petition with factual basis | MCR 3.303(B) | Sworn |
| Identity of person detained/restrained | MCR 3.303(B)(1) | Child's name |
| Nature of restraint | MCR 3.303(B)(2) | How rights restricted |
| Unlawfulness of restraint | MCR 3.303(B)(3) | Legal argument |
| Lower court's refusal to act | MCR 3.303(B)(4) | If applicable |

## U.S. District Court — Western District of Michigan

### §1983 Complaint (42 USC §1983)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Cover sheet (JS 44) | Local Rule | Civil cover sheet |
| Caption per FRCP 10(a) | FRCP 10(a) | Parties, court, case type |
| Jurisdiction statement | 28 USC §1331 | Federal question |
| Venue statement | 28 USC §1391 | Western District proper |
| Short plain statement of claim | FRCP 8(a)(2) | Each count separate |
| State actor acting under color of law | 42 USC §1983 | Essential element |
| Specific constitutional right violated | Due Process, Equal Protection | Pin cite amendment |
| Demand for relief | FRCP 8(a)(3) | Damages + declaratory + injunctive |
| Summons for each defendant | FRCP 4(b) | Clerk issues |
| Service within 90 days | FRCP 4(m) | Track deadline |

### Qualified Immunity Defeat (Pleading Stage)

| Element | Standard | Check |
|---------|----------|-------|
| Constitutional violation occurred | *Saucier v Katz*, 533 US 194 | Facts establishing violation |
| Right was clearly established | *Ashcroft v al-Kidd*, 563 US 731 | Published authority at time |
| Existing precedent on point | *Hope v Pelzer*, 536 US 730 | Similar factual scenario |
| Allegations sufficiently specific | *Iqbal*, 556 US 662 | Not conclusory |

## Judicial Tenure Commission (MCR 9.104-9.240)

### Formal Complaint (MCR 9.220)

| Requirement | Rule | Check |
|-------------|------|-------|
| Specific acts or omissions | MCR 9.220(A) | Factual allegations |
| Canon violations identified | MI Code of Judicial Conduct | Which canons breached |
| Dates, locations, case numbers | MCR 9.220(A) | Specific instances |
| Pattern evidence (if alleging) | MCR 9.220(B) | Multiple incidents |
| Verification/oath | MCR 9.220(C) | Sworn truthfulness |
| Supporting documentation | MCR 9.220(D) | Orders, transcripts, evidence |

## Citation Format Standards

### Michigan State Courts

```
Statutes:   MCL 722.23(a)
Court Rules: MCR 3.210(C)(2)
Evidence:   MRE 801(d)(2)
Cases:      People v Smith, 123 Mich App 456, 459; 789 NW2d 012 (2024)
MSC Cases:  People v Jones, 500 Mich 100, 105; 890 NW2d 345 (2022)
Canons:     Canon 2(A), Michigan Code of Judicial Conduct
```

### Federal Courts (Bluebook)

```
Statutes:   42 U.S.C. § 1983
Rules:      Fed. R. Civ. P. 8(a)(2)
Cases:      Saucier v. Katz, 533 U.S. 194, 201 (2001)
6th Cir:    Smith v. City of Detroit, 45 F.4th 123, 130 (6th Cir. 2022)
```

## Service Timing Calculator

```python
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def compute_service_deadline(hearing_date: str, method: str = "mail") -> str:
    """Compute latest date to serve motion papers."""
    hearing = datetime.strptime(hearing_date, "%Y-%m-%d")
    base_days = 9  # MCR 2.119(C)(1)
    if method == "mail":
        base_days += 3  # MCR 2.107(C)(3)
    elif method == "email":
        base_days += 1  # MCR 2.107(C)(4)
    # method == "personal" → no extra days
    service_deadline = hearing - timedelta(days=base_days)
    return service_deadline.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
```
